name: pyzotero
tagline: Python client skill wrapping pyzotero to read, write, and manage Zotero reference libraries via the Web API, local CLI, or MCP server.
verdict: Clean, transparent thin API-client skill. Credentials (ZOTERO_API_KEY etc.) are declared openly in frontmatter and stored in env vars/.env as expected for any API wrapper -- no exfiltration or hidden behavior found. Minor quality gaps: no negative-trigger guidance and no warning that create_items()/update_item() calls are not idempotent if re-run.

highlights.summary: No explicit negative-trigger guidance (when NOT to use this skill) anywhere in the file.
create_items()/update_item() examples (lines 94-106) have no note on re-run safety; re-running the create example would create duplicate items with no idempotency key mentioned.
